meta.title: Grammar Lane Expansion — Strategy Roadmap
meta.description: Six parser strategies, each pinned to a real failing baseline row, each TDD'd failing-first.

props.category: SUNNY-PC · task grammar-lane-expansion · lev validate PASS
props.title: Grammar Lane Expansion
props.subtitle: Turn the shadow Earley pilot into recovery + promotion
props.meta: Pilot S0–S5 complete | 93.6% corpus | 99 grammar candidates shadow | 0 selected

props.content: Six strategies, sequenced W1 → W2 → W4 → W5 → W3 → W6. Each pins a named failing baseline row as its functioning use case, is built failing-test-first, and ships with guard fixtures so recall never erodes precision. Corpus claims go through `just baseline` only.

props.label: Cross-cutting
props.title: Coverage is a core baseline dimension, not a variant

props.content: Every baseline run gets it. The per-row metrics **already exist** — `runner.py` attaches `candidate_metrics_from_trace()` to each row today (source coverage, parser disagreement, shadow-lift opportunity, critical-slot risk). W1 is a corpus-level **roll-up** of those plus one display line. No re-parsing, no new measurement seam, no edits to live trace producers. That is why W1 is cheap and goes first: it is the scoreboard every later strategy is judged on.

rows.fixes: None — measures all 4 families every run
rows.tdd: Fixed candidate_trace fixtures → pinned roll-up numbers; multi-segment row; zero-grammar row (denominator)
rows.gate: Parity green; no parser imports in tooling

rows.fixes: a1 adiz p02t02 'SQUAWK 4231' in ADIZ verbiage · a1 adiz p06t02 'CONTACT GROUND … FOR PARKING'
rows.tdd: 2 failing-first island fixtures (exact receipt text); 3 guard rows stay out-of-band; slot-anchor invariant
rows.gate: Islands parse w/ slots; agreement ≥ W1 snapshot

rows.fixes: 'chain the freq to to my frew' → must name FREQUENCY (today: names longest row)
rows.tdd: 1 diagnosis fixture per family; regression: crossed_final_approach NOT top partial
rows.gate: Top partial names true failing slot

rows.fixes: ads_b turn_direction_heading · ads_b maintain_heading · b1 monitor_facility_frequency — all clean 0.0, pipeline fails today
rows.tdd: Flip selection by editing register DATA only; scan: no row-ids in code; same-session pre/post receipts; clean-band check
rows.gate: Only 3 rows flip; no drift elsewhere; guards enforced

rows.fixes: b1 heading 'Turn 30 D-E-G-R-E-S right' — dehyphenate → DEGRES → priced sub → DEGREES
rows.tdd: Override-one-cost fixture moves score; fail-closed loader tests; D-E-G-R-E-S fixture; guards hold

rows.fixes: The 15 compound failing rows — largest cluster; keyword splitter is shape-blind
rows.gate: Splitter unchanged; agreement on all 15; parity

props.content:
graph LR
  W1["W1 Coverage\nscoreboard"] --> W2["W2 Island parsing\nadiz + parking"]
  W1 --> W4["W4 Partial ranking\nfrew to FREQUENCY"]
  W2 --> W5["W5 Promotion\n3 clean rows"]
  W4 --> W5
  W5 --> W3["W3 Pricing data\nD-E-G-R-E-S"]
  W2 --> W3
  W2 --> W6["W6 Segmentation\n15 compound"]
  W1 --> W6
  W5 --> DONE([First banked score bump])
props.caption: Measurement first; islands are the biggest recall lever and unlock compound; bank one narrow promotion before widening the repair surface.

props.label: STEP 1
props.title: Pin the row
props.content: Pull the exact failing utterance from the post-baseline receipt. The strategy exists to fix **that** row.

props.label: STEP 2
props.title: Fixture fails first
props.content: Unit fixture in `tests/unit/intent/grammar/` asserting the target parse/slot — red before any implementation.

props.label: STEP 3
props.title: Guard fixtures
props.content: The 3 never-promote rows get a fixture asserting they **stay** broken in-band. Recall must not erode precision.

props.label: STEP 4
props.title: Corpus roll-up
props.content: W1's coverage dimension shows the move at scale — agreement ratio must not drop. `just baseline` is the only truth.

props.label: STEP 5
props.title: Parity
props.content: `baseline-self-parity` green proves the change is deterministic and, where shadow, selection-neutral.

props.label: STEP 6
props.title: Receipt
props.content: Each slice commits a coverage-delta receipt so the win is auditable, not a prose claim.

**No rewrite.** The tooling is already factored sanely (runner 321 / display 613 / cli 487 / compare 282 LOC) and the per-row metric source of truth correctly lives in `pipeline_results.py`. The minimal right move: add one `tooling/baseline/coverage.py` that rolls up existing per-row `candidate_metrics` into a run-level `grammar_coverage` block, wire one call into runner's RunReport assembly, render one line in `display.py`. W5 also extends `compare.py` with a per-segment selected-winner mode (the multi-segment bug caught this session). That is the whole tooling footprint — additive, not a refactor of what works.
